visual_description: Two men stand on a balcony at night; one in a light shirt gestures while holding his head, the other in a dark suit faces him. Behind them, a building with lit windows and a wall with peeling paint are visible. The man in the light shirt leans slightly forward, his gaze directed toward the other man, while the suited man stands upright, facing him.
audio_description: The audio clip begins with a sharp, metallic click, likely the sound of a latch or handle being operated, immediately followed by a low-frequency mechanical hum and a faint high-frequency hiss. These background noises suggest the presence of an active engine or machinery, possibly from a vehicle or industrial setting, and indicate a moderately reverberant, enclosed space. A male voice, speaking in standard Mandarin with a neutral northern accent, is heard close to the microphone. He says, “我没时间回去吃饭了” (“I don’t have time to go back for a meal”), his tone calm and matter-of-fact, with a slight downward inflection. The speech is clear and intelligible, with no or urgency. After a brief pause, the same speaker utters a short, neutral “啊” (“ah”), serving as a conversational filler. The ambient hum and hiss persist, and the acoustic environment remains consistent, reinforcing the impression of a utilitarian, enclosed space such as a vehicle cabin or industrial room.
